title: Dorstenia houstonii
intro: Dorstenia houstonii, also known as Houston's croton or Hottentots' fig, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities, which may aid in treating wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dorstenia houstonii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, with the most common herbal preparations being a tea made from the leaves and roots, as well as a topical cream or ointment for skin and wound treatment.
taxonomy: Dorstenia houstonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Moraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Dorstenia, which consists of various species of tropical plants. The Moraceae family includes around 40 genera of plants, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. Further classification of the plant may also include its kingdom and phylum, as a part of the broader botanical classification system.
